ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the weather station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station */,
  "station_type" TEXT /* Type of weather station */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Installation date of the weather station */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data entry */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station that provided the data */,
  "temperature_c" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity as a percentage */,
  "wind_speed_kmh" REAL /* Wind speed in kilometers per hour */,
  "wind_bearing_degrees" REAL /* Wind bearing in degrees */,
  "visibility_km" REAL /* Visibility in kilometers */,
  "pressure_millibars" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure in millibars */,
  "rain" INTEGER /* Amount of rain in millimeters */,
  "dew_point" REAL /* Dew point in degrees Celsius */,
  "cloud_cover" REAL /* Cloud cover as a percentage */,
  "solar_radiation" REAL /* Solar radiation in watts per square meter */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Data quality flag */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Data source */,
  "observation_date" TEXT /* Date of the observation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition (e.g., Cold, Warm, Normal) */,
  "classification" TEXT /* Weather type classification */,
  "subtype" TEXT /* Weather type subtype */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, data analyst, admin) */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Access method */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "observations" (
  "observation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observation */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the corresponding weather data entry */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments regarding the observation */,
  "reported_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reported the observation */,
  "observation_type" TEXT /* Type of observation */,
  "observation_method" TEXT /* Method of observation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("observation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observations_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecasting_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecasting model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the forecasting model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model's methodology */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of forecasting model */,
  "model_resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the forecasting model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecasting model */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., accuracy, precision) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of performance metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_performance_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES forecasting_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station that issued the alert */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert (e.g., severe weather warning) */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date the alert was issued */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Level of the alert */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Expiration date of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather event */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station where the event occurred */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of weather event (e.g., storm, flood) */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date of the event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "event_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the event */,
  "event_impact" TEXT /* Impact of the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_events_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)
